final_prompt.prompt:
Create an original illustration of the Lantern Festival night panorama in Tang Dynasty Chang'an city, rendered as a traditional Chinese gongbi (工笔重彩) handscroll painting.

Style/medium: gongbi heavy-color (工笔重彩) handscroll (长卷) painting, meticulous brushwork in the tradition of Tang court illustration, rich mineral pigments on silk
Composition: sweeping horizontal panoramic scroll format, wide establishing view of the entire city boulevard, layered depth from foreground crowd to distant city gates and towers
Subject: tens of thousands of hanging and floating lanterns (万盏花灯) in varied shapes — lotus, fish, dragon, palace lanterns — illuminating the night sky above the grand avenues of Chang'an
Scene/backdrop: Tang Dynasty Chang'an city at night during the Lantern Festival (元宵灯会), grand vermilion city walls and multi-eaved watchtowers in the distance, dense festive crowds in Tang-era court and folk costumes lining the streets
Lighting/mood: warm amber and golden glow radiating from myriad lanterns, soft pools of light overlapping across the scene, celebratory and magnificent atmosphere
Color palette: deep indigo night sky as ground, warm gold and vermilion lantern light as dominant accent, malachite green and azurite blue in architectural details, white lead highlights on silk garments
Materials/textures: fine silk ground with visible weave, layered mineral pigment with subtle granular texture, gold-ink outlines on lantern tassels and architectural eaves
Avoid: Western oil painting rendering, photorealistic lens effects, modern lighting flares, flat cartoon style, anachronistic elements
